name: Mojhy Apples
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash and dry the apples. Remove stems and replace them with stick stuck halfway into the apple.
2. Combine corn syrup, sugar, and water in a deep saucepan. Stir and cook over medium heat until thoroughly dissolved and mixture boils. Then cook without stirring to soft crack stage (a small amount of the mixture separates into threads which are hard but not brittle when dropped in cold water; or candy thermometer registers 280°F). Remove from heat; add extract and stir only enough to mix.
3. Place pan over boiling water to keep syrup from thickening.
4. Dip apples and wind in a circular motion until thoroughly covered with syrup.
5. Place on buttered pan with sticks upright and cool.
Makes 6 Mojif Apples.
